original_post.author: Lea Newkirk
original_post.title: ◀️Moving Backward
original_post.body: 4Lea Newkirk3d • Holistic Self-Improvement 📚◀️Moving BackwardI’m still sitting with what @Scott Northwolf taught on the call about Reverse Engineering our goals, and honestly, it’s already changing my life. Instead of looking at where I am now and trying to climb the mountain toward a goal, I’ve been practicing sitting in the frequency of already having achieved it, and then looking back at the steps I took to get there. It shifts everything from "I hope I can do this" to "it is already a done." It makes the path feel so much more inevitable and way less overwhelming.  I’m curious, what’s one goal you’re currently reverse-engineering? If you look back from the "finished" version of your goal, what was the very first step you took to get there?11Last comment 2d ago

original_post.author: Lea Newkirk
original_post.title: Protecting the Focus.
original_post.body: 4Lea Newkirk2d • Mindset 🧠Protecting the Focus.I’ve been reading Deep Work by Cal Newport (recommended by @Scott Northwolf ), and it’s shifted how I look at the goals we’ve been reverse-engineering together. It made me realize that if we want to hit those big targets we talk about on our Friday calls, we can't afford to have our attention fractured. We’re all putting in so much work to stay in a high-frequency state, but I’ve noticed how easily "busy-ness" and multitasking can pull us right back down into the noise. To manifest the version of life we’re aiming for, I’m learning that we have to be just as intentional with our focus as we are with our vision.  I’m curious, what’s your take on this? Do you feel like multitasking is a necessary skill for where we're going, or is it just a distraction from the deep work we're actually here to do?11Last comment 2d ago

original_post.title: Don't try to escape 😵‍💫
original_post.body: 5Scott Northwolf12d • Mindset 🧠Don't try to escape 😵‍💫If you are trying to escape the present moment you are failing in real time.  The masculine error lies on believing that things will be fundamentally different some day.  "Someday my women will stop complaining."  "Someday I'll make enough money and I'll finally be free."  "Someday I'll land a client and everything will change forever."  Guess what?  When you land a client...  You still got to keep doing outreach.  You still got to keep building upwards.  I know because it happened to me.  Don't make the mistake of trying to escape your current situation.  If you stay on this path for long enough you WILL become rich and you WILL miss the days in which you were struggling and striving to hit these goals.  Enjoy the process now.  I know it sounds like a cliche but if you do that and let go of all atatchment to the outcome the inputs will become effortless and everything will flow without frustration. original_post.title: Food quality in restaurants: probably not for you
original_post.body: 3Andrew Brooks🔥Feb 24 • General 💭Food quality in restaurants: probably not for youHow often do you go out, eat somewhere, and think to yourself "well, that wasn't great." And how did you feel afterwards?  There are reasons for the generally low quality of food in most restaurants. Mostly they are limited by their food budget. And the food budget limits the quality they are able to purchase because we also generally want cheap food. What that means is that most of the produce will be conventional produce, not organic. The meats (a large part of the budget) will be of lower quality and the rest will be medium quality at best.  The things we WANT: organic, fresh, healthy, and delicious, simply are not available within the cheap food framework. Unfortunately to get the good stuff you have to pay more. That's just the way it is for now...31Last comment Feb 24

Radical change only happens when the pain of staying the same outweights the pain of change.  There's a certain kind of restlessness that is required for such change to take place. Sleepless nights, doubts, fears... frustration? Meeting with reality and having to accept the world is simply NOT how you thought it was. So what will you do now? Take it as insight and modify your behavoir to actually get what you want or stay down, in regret, failure...  People think Self-Improvement is a walk of roses, all about positive mentality and some soft meditation but this is a FUCKING WAR TO THE DEATH with your old self.  There's no pleasure without pain and too much pleasure in the end will bring more pain than you could ever had imagine.  What do you really want out of this life? original_post.title: Basic Leadership Primer
original_post.body: 2Paul HedronJan 24 • General 💭Basic Leadership PrimerThe basic components of a team are as follows:  A group of individuals, a vision, and a facilitator to direct effort and attention.  A leader must be an expert in the nature of the vision, the individuals on the team, and the conditions in which the vision and team interact.  Without a vision or goal, the collection of individuals cannot coalesce. Without individuals to coalesce, a vision or goal is without potential and impetus. Without understanding of the conditions, a team will experience unnecessary inertia, spending effort to achieve the vision.  A vision is merely a blueprint, something to build. The individuals on the team are the builders. The leader is the architect and facilitator.  The base component of a team and shared vision is the individual. Honestly, I’ve been in pain my whole life for not having a “stable” friend group, is this normal?  My friends circle we’re only 3 at a time, and it would die off overtime.  Currently, having not deep relationships with my school friends.  There’s so many people who I can be friends with in school, it’s too easy.  But I have a limiting belief that, theyre not worth it. original_post.title: Be full of yourself, be delusional.
original_post.body: 2Laurits Valentin Offersen🔥Jan 21 • Mindset 🧠Be full of yourself, be delusional.I’m 18 years old and I’m fully aware that, on paper, I’m not that impressive yet.  I don’t have crazy money.I’m not a finished product.I’m still early.  But here’s the part most people won’t admit:  I’m delusional about where I’m going.  Not in an ego way, in a commitment way.I know I’m getting there because I’ve already decided I won’t stop.  That belief is what gets me up at 5 am  That belief is what keeps me working when no one’s watching.  That belief is what makes quitting impossible.  People call it arrogance.  I call it certainty.  If I didn’t believe that strongly in my future self, I’d move like everyone else, procrastinating, Hesitating and Half-committed.  But I don’t need proof yet.  I just need to stay consistent.  Being “delusional” is only a problem when you don’t work.When you do the work, it becomes inevitable.  if you do average ammounts of work you become average  if you do exceptional

original_post.author: Brandon Maloney
original_post.title: Does anyone know how to become ambitious?
original_post.body: 5Brandon Maloney2d • 🤝NetworkingDoes anyone know how to become ambitious?I've realized recently I'm not obsessed like I used to be with things. I would say I'm more productive, smarter, more skilled, but the absolute OBSESSION I had with sports, or training, or work, just simply isn't there to the degree it used to be at the moment.  My question to you is, how do you WANT more. How do you give yourself big goals that almost scare you, that make you crave working on them?  (Also funny picture of my when I was in middle school)2541New comment 9h ago

comment.author: Zakh Chowdhury
comment.content: I've consumed 100s of books, podcasts, articles, and courses on neuroscience, metaphyiscs, psychology, and manifestation and here is my viewpoint on developing ambition:Most people think they lack ambition, but what they actually lack is clarity, and those are two very different problems. Ambition isn’t something you force, it’s something that naturally shows up when you’re moving toward the right thing. The issue is that most of us are chasing goals that were never really ours in the first plac... See more

replies.content: @Zakh Chowdhury thank you a ton for the feedback, this was honestly a difficult read for me because I'm a lil slow, but here's what I got from it:Find out what you wantFind the clearest path to get itBe authenticLet your pulls pull youDoes that sound accurate?

comment.author: Harry Montford
comment.content: What worked for me was increasing my intrinsic motivation. It might sound super weird but this is usually what happens when we try to reach our goals:Extrinsic motivation sparks action → Action sparks flow → Flow boosts intrinsic motivation.Extrinsic motivation is things outside of us like money fame etc. When we take action, that puts us in flow state and that boosts our intrinsic motivation which is our internal reasons for doing something.What really helped me increase my intrinsic motivation is having clear goals and a clear why like the above comments said as well :)

original_post.author: Michael Pluszek
original_post.title: Soul vs Identity
original_post.body: 8Michael Pluszek🔥16d • 💅OtherSoul vs IdentityI posted a question about identity earlier in Synthesizer community, and it sparked some great conversations and perspectives.  Now another question came up that I’m curious about.  What is identity… and what is soul?  What’s the difference?  And how does a person actually know what is what?  Would love to hear your reflections and perspectives.52174New comment 17h ago

comment.author: Michael Pluszek
comment.content: I truly love these conversations, as reading your perspectives, helps me unlock new things. Thank you.

replies.author: Kenneth Khamphiphone
replies.content: @Lexi Cruz the idea that identity evolves but the soul stays constant is a strong frame. Almost like the soul is the stability that makes changing your identity safe.

replies.author: Lexi Cruz
replies.content: @Kenneth Khamphiphone I like that frame! If the soul provides the stability, it would explain why identity can evolve so much throughout life. It makes identity flexible because something deeper is holding everything together. Do you think people are aware of that stability, or only notice it after big identity shifts?

replies.author: Michael Pluszek
replies.content: @Lexi Cruz great question.I am very curious from your experience what is the soul then and where does it come from?

replies.author: Kenneth Khamphiphone
replies.content: @Lexi Cruz That's the part that makes identity less fragile once you see it. If the soul holds steady then losing an identity isn't losing yourself. More like a costume change than an ending.

replies.author: Lexi Cruz
replies.content: @Michael Pluszek The way I’ve been thinking about it is that identity is the character we build through roles and experiences, while the soul is the awareness behind that character. I’m still exploring where it comes from though. What’s your view? Do you see the soul as something spiritual, or more like awareness?

replies.author: Lexi Cruz
replies.content: @Kenneth Khamphiphone I like that way of describing it! Seeing identity as the character and the soul as the witness makes identity feel much less rigid. The character can evolve without feeling like you are losing yourself. It makes me wonder if a lot of identity struggles come from people thinking the character is the whole self, when it might just be one layer.

replies.author: Michael Pluszek
replies.content: @Lexi Cruz great way to explain the identity part.The soul seems to be more difficult to explain.The best way I can share how I see it/experience it as I sit here right now would be like comparing it to consciousnes.It's that deep part behind it all.

original_post.author: Mark Santos
original_post.title: What I learned after hitting 800 subscribers
original_post.body: 7Mark Santos29d • 🤑 MonetizationWhat I learned after hitting 800 subscribersHey Synthesizers!  During the fall of 2024 I uploaded 7 videos for 12 weeks straight and ended up going from 200 -> 800 subscribers.  I did this while being a 27 year old married man, working a full time job.  After hitting 800 subscribers I noticed three things: - I was unsure about what to sell - I didn’t have a single client - I only booked one call  I was confused about what to do next.  I hopped on Skool and was reminded about a beautiful thing that exists.  “The Synthesizer Roadmap”  I put my trust in Andrew Kirby and hopped the following 5 months on 100 calls - 65 networking calls - 30 discovery calls - 5 sales calls  As a result I closed 2 online clients on Synthesized deals.  That’s because The Roadmap allowed me to get on calls and discover real life problems.  This is where I could leverage my proof in multiple areas and create customized solutions.  How far are you into The Roadmap are you right no

original_post.author: Aathraey Shrikanth
original_post.title: When do you check your phone?
original_post.body: 7Aathraey Shrikanth🔥17d • 💅OtherWhen do you check your phone?Besides turning off your alarm, when's the first time that you check your phone?  I used to have a period where I went no phone until 6 pm; those were some of my most productive days ever.  Coincidence? I'm not so sure about that. So when's the earliest that you check your phone?40137New comment 2d ago

original_post.author: Lea Newkirk
original_post.title: 🌒Synthesis Requires Silence.
original_post.body: 6Lea Newkirk8d • 💅Other🌒Synthesis Requires Silence. We often think of rest as a "reward" for being productive (most times I still do). I am starting to realize that’s just bad math. In the high-intensity environment of self-improvement, where we are constantly upgrading our internal software, if I don’t schedule the off time, my nervous system just schedules a burnout for me. Rest isn't the opposite of productivity, it’s the essential cooldown phase that allows the next sprint to happen. I mean, we wouldn't run a high-end processor at 100% capacity 24/7, it would melt. Our biology is no different. I’m curious, in an environment like this, how do you personally distinguish between "healthy friction" and a nervous system that’s starting to redline, and what are the specific warning signs you usually miss until it’s too late?2234New comment 2d ago

original_post.author: Piotr Rzepecki
original_post.title: Why Most People Don’t Get Results (Simple Fix)
original_post.body: 5Piotr Rzepecki7d • 💅OtherWhy Most People Don’t Get Results (Simple Fix)Most people don’t fail from lack of knowledge. They fail from lack of action.  Facts: 👉 70-90% of people never finish courses. 👉 Most never launch anything. 👉 First attempt is usually bad, but necessary.  What actually works: 1. Launch fast First version in 24-72h  2. Talk to people Send 20 messages Goal is 3-5 real responses  3. Post daily Even simple posts build momentum  4. Improve based on feedback Not on your own ideas  5. Repeat Every week, not once  That’s it No results = no volume More action = more feedback = better results Start now Fix later138New comment 6d ago

original_post.author: Cris Morales
original_post.title: Networking Fatigue?
original_post.body: 7Cris Morales14d • 🤝NetworkingNetworking Fatigue? I'm all in the strategy of comments, chats, posts, dms, etc. But I do sometimes go to make my daily contributions in a group, and end up leaving a "congrats man!". Sometimes, there is nothing else to say.  Sometimes, groups aren't super active, or they aren't talking about what I know I can help them with. My question to myself and to you guys is  If there is nothing to say, should we even say it? Or should we ALWAYS be making ourselves visible, chatting, commenting, posting in the group, even though it doesn't have much to do with your niche, offer, solution, etc?4381New comment 7d ago

comment.author: Lexi Cruz
comment.content: Good question! I think the goal isn’t just visibility, it’s contribution. If there’s nothing meaningful to add, forcing a comment can start to feel transactional. When you genuinely help someone or add a thoughtful perspective, that’s when people actually remember you.That’s also why Synthesizer has standards around comment quality. If everyone posted low-effort comments just to stay visible, it would change the energy of the community. I'm curious how others think about that balance.

replies.content: @Lexi Cruz true. been frustrated actually with the comments in the community which are just made out of pure transactional intent. You felt that? I am guilty of it cuz I did that too, I'm not perfect, and then I felt its not worth putting a mask, so quit that bad habit.

comment.author: Benedek Santa
comment.content: Yeah, might be a controversial take mostly on skool, but I generally believe like if you have nothing useful to say don't say it. I view every message I have as a digital billboard, a digital advertisement for myself. And if I'm associating myself with low quality content, then I rather don't even want to put that out. I want every comment someone reads from me to feel high quality, thoughtful and intentional. Not just a low quality something, because every message you leave signals to people what type of consultant creator and synthesizer you are.

original_post.author: Harry Montford
original_post.title: What is your ideal "deep work archetype"?
original_post.body: 7Harry Montford🔥15d • 💅OtherWhat is your ideal "deep work archetype"?We have 4 main deep work archetypes.  These are like the 4 classes you can pick inside of a video game. Each have their own benefits, buffs and weaknesses.  They are: - Monastic: 1 main task that you work on for most of the day, minimal breaks. - Bidomal: Long deep work days, but also complete rest days inside the week.  - Rhythmic: Deep work sessions in the morning, shallow work in the afternoon -> evening.  - Journalistic: No clear structure, one minute you could be partying in Bali, the next you are recording          a YT video.  My personal favourite is Rhythmic, but I also know many people who prefer the Monastic work type.  SO here's my question, what would do you follow and which one would you LIKE to try?1639New comment 12d ago
